conditionsHeading.title: What powered cold therapy does that ice packs can't
conditionsHeading.lead: Cold therapy is one of the most evidence-supported recovery tools in modern orthopaedics.

conditions.title: Consistent temperature
conditions.description: A powered unit maintains the target therapeutic temperature for hours — ice packs warm up within 20 minutes.

conditions.title: Active compression
conditions.description: Cycling compression pumps swelling out of the surgical area — reducing recovery time.

conditions.title: Less pain medication
conditions.description: Clinical studies show patients using powered cold therapy need significantly less post-op opioid medication.

conditions.title: Faster return-to-function

seoContent.title: Cold therapy after surgery — what the evidence says
seoContent.intro: Cold therapy isn't new — but powered cold therapy with compression is fundamentally different from ice packs. Here's why surgeons increasingly prescribe it.

sections.heading: Consistent cold vs intermittent ice
sections.body: Therapeutic cooling needs to stay in the right temperature range — too cold causes tissue damage, too warm does nothing. Powered units maintain the right range for hours; ice packs warm within 20 minutes, often well before the user notices.

sections.heading: Compression matters
sections.body: Swelling is a major driver of post-op pain and slow recovery. Active compression — pumped cycling pressure — moves swelling out of the surgical area in a way that static wraps cannot.

sections.heading: When to use cold therapy
sections.body: Most useful in the first 5–14 days after surgery, and in acute soft-tissue injury. Less helpful for chronic conditions like OA. Your surgeon will advise on timing.

seoContent.disclaimer: Educational content. Follow your surgeon's protocol for cold therapy duration and frequency.

faq.question: How long do I use cold therapy each day?
faq.answer: Typically 20–30 minute sessions, 3–6 times daily in the first week post-op. Your surgeon will give specific guidance.

faq.question: Can I sleep with the unit on?
faq.answer: Some patients do, but most surgeons recommend timed sessions to avoid prolonged cooling. The unit has timers for safety.

faq.question: How long do I need the cold therapy unit for?
faq.answer: Most patients use it intensively for 1–2 weeks post-op, with reducing frequency over 4–6 weeks total. Rental can be a sensible option.
